introSection: Runaway Mountain is a Premier Rides enclosed roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. It opened in 1996, the same year as Skull Mountain at Six Flags Great Adventure.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Runaway Mountain opened to the public on June 12, 1996. It was the first Premier Rides roller coaster to open. It replaced Spinnaker, which was relocated to Six Flags Fiesta Texas in 1996.

The ride received a new entrance sign in 2021.

In 2022, a single rider line was added to Runaway Mountain.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride is an enclosed roller coaster made to look as if it takes place in a dark cave. The queue line is themed to resemble a mineshaft.

sections.title: Station
sections.content: Runaway Mountain's station has separate areas for loading and unloading.

fullText:
Runaway Mountain is a Premier Rides enclosed roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. It opened in 1996, the same year as Skull Mountain at Six Flags Great Adventure.

Runaway Mountain opened to the public on June 12, 1996. It was the first Premier Rides roller coaster to open. It replaced Spinnaker, which was relocated to Six Flags Fiesta Texas in 1996.

The ride received a new entrance sign in 2021.

In 2022, a single rider line was added to Runaway Mountain.

The ride is an enclosed roller coaster made to look as if it takes place in a dark cave. The queue line is themed to resemble a mineshaft.

Runaway Mountain's station has separate areas for loading and unloading.

Black track and supports.
